products.title: Sharpening Rod - Pin Series (X02O)
products.body_html:
Your Pin Series knives aren't dull. They're crooked. After a week of regular cutting, the microscopic edge folds over and the blade starts pushing through food instead of slicing it clean. This diamond-coated honing rod realigns that edge in two minutes, without grinding away steel the way a whetstone does. Run it before dinner, not after the damage is done.

Honing, Not Sharpening

There's a difference worth knowing. Sharpening grinds away steel to form a new edge. Honing straightens the edge you already have. The fine diamond surface catches the folded-over edge and pushes it back into line with a few strokes per side. Do it every few days and the blade stays sharp between stone sessions, rather than slowly degrading until it won't bite through a tomato skin. Works on all knives in the Pin Series, and on any other kitchen knife in a similar hardness bracket.

Specifications

  • Material: Diamond-coated high carbon steel rod, chrome plated
  • Handle: Natural olive wood with stainless steel rivets
  • Use: Quick edge realignment between whetstone sessions on the Pin Series and any 60+ HRC kitchen knife.
  • Series: Pin Series (X02O)
  • Model: X02O-MDB

products.body_html:
Reach for this between sessions, not just when something feels dull. A honing rod doesn't sharpen a blade - it realigns the edge that's folded over from ordinary use. Five or six passes before a prep session and your knife cuts the way it did the week you bought it. This one matches the Uliassi Series in handle material and finish, so it lives on the block without looking out of place.

Diamond Particles, Not Just Steel

The rod is 430 stainless steel coated with diamond particles, which means it can both realign a rolled edge and lightly refine it on the same pass. It works on any of the X03 series knives, including the powder steel core blades at 62 HRC - hardness that a plain steel honing rod can struggle to grip. Diamond abrasive bites into hard steel where a smooth rod just slides. The rosewood handle matches the rest of the Uliassi Series and the 23 cm rod length gives enough working surface for the 10-inch chef and carving knives.

Specifications

products.title: Vegetable Tan Leather Doubled-Sided Strop
products.body_html:
This is the final step to a truly "scary sharp" knife. If you’ve sharpened your blade on a stone, the Xinzuo Leather Strop Paddle is the secret to taking that edge from just "sharp" to a razor-fine, mirror polish that glides through food.

A Simple, Two-Step Pro Finish

We’ve made the professional stropping process easy. This double-sided paddle gives you two distinct surfaces for a perfect, polished edge:

  • The Refining Side: Comes pre-loaded with premium KOYO green compound to remove the final, microscopic burr left after sharpening.
  • The Polishing Side: A smooth, 10,000-grit equivalent surface of pure, hand-cut vegetable-tanned cowhide that delivers that final, gleaming mirror finish.

Built for a lifetime of use, the solid North American walnut handle provides a stable, comfortable grip, giving you the perfect angle and pressure for consistent results.

  • Type: Double-Sided Leather Strop
  • Paddle: Solid North American Walnut
  • Leather: Hand-cut, vegetable-tanned cowhide
  • Compound Side: Pre-loaded with KOYO green compound
  • Leather Side: Smooth cowhide (10,000 grit equivalent)
  • Stropping Surface: 205mm long x 65mm wide
  • Total Length: 340mm (including handle)
  • Weight: 265g
  • Model: XZ-DDB02

Complete Your Sharpening Kit

Pair this strop with a 1000/6000 water stone. Use the stone for shaping and honing, then finish on this strop for a razor-ready edge on all your favorite knives.

products.title: Wooden Knife Sheath for Boning Knife (TG01)
products.body_html:
A boning knife's edge is too narrow and too aggressive to survive rattling loose in a drawer. Reach for this saya after every session, slide it on, and the blade goes back into storage properly sheathed. Ash wood, internal magnets, dry fit every time.

Built Around a Narrow Profile

Boning knives are thinner and more tapered than a chef knife, so the sheath opening has to match or you get wobble. TG01 is shaped for blades in the 15-16 cm range, with a narrow channel that follows the typical boning profile from heel to tip. The internal magnets grip the spine firmly, which matters on a narrow blade where there's less surface to seat against. It doesn't need a pin to stay shut.

Specifications

  • Material: Ash wood with internal magnetic retention
  • Compatible knife: Boning knife, approx. 15-16 cm (6-6.3") blade
  • Use: Drawer storage and transport protection for boning knives
  • Model: TG01

This sheath is sized to match the blade profile of the following Xinzuo knives. Check the code in your product title (e.g. Chef Knife - Lan Series (B37)) against this list.

products.title: Wooden Knife Sheath for Bread Knife (MB01)
products.body_html:
The serrated edge on a bread knife is impossible to resharpen at home if it gets damaged, so protecting it matters more than on any other blade in your kitchen. This wooden saya keeps the teeth clear of drawer contact and safe for the next loaf. Don't skip it.

Sized for a Long, Serrated Blade

Bread knives are longer than most, and the serrations mean you can't just press-check the edge like you would a chef knife. The ash wood sheath runs the full length of a standard bread knife blade, with the opening wide enough to clear the serration tips as you slide it on dry. Internal magnets hold the spine of the blade firmly once it's seated, so it won't work loose in transit.

products.title: Xinzuo Canvas & Leather Chef Apron
products.body_html:
This isn't just an apron; it is your daily armor. Designed for professional chefs who spend hours on their feet, the Xinzuo Professional Apron combines rugged durability with ergonomic comfort. Gone are the days of thin fabric and neck strain—this is built for the heat of the kitchen.

Zero Neck Strain

The standout feature is the ergonomic cross-back strap design. Unlike traditional aprons that hang heavy on your neck, this system distributes the weight evenly across your shoulders and back. You can wear it for a 12-hour shift without feeling that familiar ache, allowing you to focus entirely on your prep.

Premium Materials

Built to match the quality of your knives, this apron is crafted from thick, high-quality canvas that resists heat and spills. The straps and accents are made from genuine full-grain leather, adding a layer of rugged sophistication that only looks better with age. It features robust metal clasps and grommets for secure fastening.

Smart Storage

Efficiency is key. The apron features varied pockets specifically designed for the tools of the trade. Keep your phone safe in the chest pocket, and have your tasting spoons, tweezers, and notepad instantly accessible in the spacious waist pockets.

products.title: Xinzuo Premium Leather Vintage Knife Roll Bag
products.body_html:
Your knives are an investment; they deserve better than being thrown into a backpack wrapped in a tea towel. The Xinzuo Premium "Vintage" Knife Roll is the ultimate travel companion for the professional chef or serious enthusiast. It combines rugged durability with an aesthetic that only gets better with time.

Crazy Horse Leather & Waxed Canvas

This roll is crafted from heavy-duty 16oz Waxed Canvas, which naturally repels water and stains—perfect for the messy reality of a working kitchen. The straps, handle, and cover flap are made from genuine Crazy Horse Leather (Full Grain). This specific leather is famous for its toughness and its ability to develop a rich, unique patina as it ages, telling the story of your culinary journey.

Maximum Capacity

Designed for the chef who travels with a full arsenal, this roll features 12 pockets in total. You have 10 dedicated knife slots capable of holding blades up to 17 inches in length (handling even your longest slicers), plus zippered utility pockets for your tweezers, tasting spoons, and notepad.

Travel Ready

Whether you are commuting to the restaurant or heading to a BBQ, carry it your way. The roll features a reinforced leather carry handle and includes a detachable, adjustable shoulder strap for hands-free transport.

  • Material: 16oz Waxed Canvas + Full Grain "Crazy Horse" Leather
  • Capacity: 10 Knife Slots + 2 Utility Pockets (12 Total)
  • Max Knife Length: Fits knives up to ~43cm (17")
  • Dimensions (Unrolled): ~90cm x 43cm
  • Features: Shoulder strap, interior leather flap guard, brass hardware.
  • Color: Vintage Brown

Care Instructions

Do not machine wash. Spot clean canvas with a damp cloth. The leather can be treated with leather conditioner annually to maintain its suppleness.

collections.title: ACCESSORIES
collections.body_html:
Complete your kitchen setup with Xinzuo's range of premium knife accessories. From magnetic knife holders and walnut knife blocks to end-grain chopping boards and professional sharpening tools, every accessory is designed to protect your blades and improve your workflow.

Our collection includes acacia and walnut chopping boards in multiple sizes, magnetic wall-mounted knife racks, countertop knife blocks, honing steel rods, and Japanese whetstones for maintaining a razor-sharp edge at home.

Pair these accessories with any Xinzuo knife series for the complete kitchen experience. All accessories ship Australia-wide with free delivery on orders over $100.

image.alt: ACCESSORIES - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: B27 Yi Series
collections.body_html:
The Xinzuo B27 Yi Series embodies precision craftsmanship and timeless elegance. Featuring a 67-layer Damascus blade paired with a refined Rosewood handle, this collection delivers professional-grade performance wrapped in a classic, balanced design suited for both modern and traditional kitchens.

High-Performance 10Cr15CoMoV Core

At the core of each blade lies premium 10Cr15CoMoV high-carbon stainless steel, widely recognized as a VG-10 equivalent. Engineered for excellent edge retention, corrosion resistance, and durability, the blade is vacuum heat-treated and deep frozen to achieve a hardness of 60±2 HRC.

The result is a razor-sharp edge that glides effortlessly through vegetables, meats, and delicate ingredients while maintaining long-lasting sharpness under daily use.

The 67-layer folded Damascus construction enhances structural strength while creating the distinctive flowing pattern that reflects true artisanal craftsmanship.

Classic Rosewood Handle with Riveted Construction

The handle is crafted from premium Rosewood, selected for its density, stability, and rich reddish tones. The full-tang, triple-riveted construction ensures strength, durability, and perfect balance between blade and handle.

Ergonomically shaped for comfort and control, the B27 handle offers a secure grip and reduced fatigue during extended prep sessions, making it ideal for both professional chefs and passionate home cooks.

Key Features

Core Steel: 10Cr15CoMoV (VG-10 equivalent) for superior sharpness and edge retention
Blade Construction: 67-layer Damascus steel for strength and corrosion resistance
Hardness: 60±2 HRC (Vacuum Heat Treated & Deep Freeze)
Handle: Triple-riveted Rosewood with full-tang structure
Design: Balanced, elegant profile for precision and control

The B27 Yi Series is designed for those who value classic aesthetics, structural integrity, and uncompromising cutting performance in every slice.

image.alt: B27 Yi Series - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: B30 Master Series
collections.body_html:
The Xinzuo B30 Master Series is built for precision, power, and professional confidence. Explore our full range of professional chef knives. Combining a 67-layer Damascus blade with a forged sycamore wood handle, this collection delivers refined cutting performance with a bold, modern presence designed for serious kitchens.

High-Performance 10Cr15CoMoV Core

At the heart of the blade lies premium 10Cr15CoMoV high-carbon stainless steel, widely regarded as a VG-10 equivalent. Engineered for exceptional edge retention, toughness, and corrosion resistance, the blade is vacuum heat-treated and deep frozen to achieve a hardness of 60±2 HRC. The result is a razor-sharp edge that slices cleanly through meat, vegetables, and delicate ingredients with precision and control.

The 67-layer Damascus construction enhances strength and durability while showcasing a striking wave pattern that reflects craftsmanship and attention to detail.

Forged Sycamore Wood Handle

The handle is crafted from stabilized sycamore wood, selected for its durability and natural character. Forged and shaped for optimal balance, it provides a secure, comfortable grip during extended prep work. The smooth finish highlights the wood’s unique grain while ensuring ergonomic control and reduced hand fatigue.

Key Features

The B30 Master Series is designed for chefs who demand performance without compromise , a collection that blends technical excellence with confident, modern craftsmanship.

image.alt: B30 Master Series - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: B37 Lan Series
collections.body_html:
The Xinzuo B37 Lan Series steps up from the standard Damascus range by pairing a 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel core with a warm, pale olive wood handle. It is the series for cooks who want the edge retention and hardness of a powder steel blade without the formal, traditional aesthetic of a rosewood-handled knife. Explore the full Damascus knife collection to see how the Lan Series sits relative to the rest of the lineup.

Premium 14Cr14MoVNb Powder Steel Core

The blade core is 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel, produced through powder metallurgy rather than conventional ingot casting. The process begins with pre-alloyed steel powder that is sintered under controlled pressure, resulting in a far more uniform carbide distribution than a conventionally forged steel of similar composition. That fine, consistent grain is what allows the B37 to reach 62-64 HRC through vacuum heat treatment without the brittleness risk that hardness level would carry in a coarser steel. In practical terms, it means a blade that takes a sharper angle on the whetstone and holds that angle longer through daily prep work. The same alloy is used in the X03 Uliassi Series, which pushes toward professional kitchen requirements with a darker rosewood handle and hidden tang; the Lan Series takes the same core steel in a different direction, with a full-tang construction and the lighter character of olive wood.

73-Layer Damascus Cladding

The powder steel core is enclosed in 73 layers of Damascus cladding, six layers more than the 67-layer construction used across the standard Xinzuo Damascus series. The additional folds produce a denser, more tightly patterned surface visible along the flats of the blade, and the extra cladding layers add chip resistance at the shoulders where the softer outer steel meets the harder core. Each blade is hand-finished to bring out the contrast between the layers. For more on what distinguishes genuine Damascus construction from cosmetic patterns, see the Damascus knives guide.

Polished Olive Wood Handle, Full Tang

The handle is shaped from olive wood, a dense hardwood with a fine grain and a pale golden tone marked by subtle darker streaks. Compared to the deep reddish-brown of rosewood, olive wood reads lighter and more neutral, giving the Lan Series a clean, almost Scandinavian character that pairs well with the Damascus blade without competing for visual attention. The full-tang construction runs the blade steel the full length of the handle for structural integrity and a solid, balanced feel in hand. Each handle is individually polished, and because olive wood grain is never identical between trees, no two Lan Series knives will look exactly alike.

Key Features

  • Core Steel: 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel for uniform grain structure, finer edge geometry, and superior edge retention
  • Blade Construction: 73-layer Damascus cladding for chip resistance and a dense surface pattern
  • Hardness: 62-64 HRC (Vacuum Heat Treated)
  • Handle: Polished olive wood, full-tang construction for balance and structural integrity
  • Design: Pale, natural aesthetic with powder steel edge quality, suited to modern kitchen settings

The B37 Lan Series is built for cooks who want professional-grade cutting performance from a powder steel core and prefer a lighter, more natural handle over the darker tones of traditional rosewood or ebony.

image.alt: LAN Series - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: BBQ & Grilling Knives
collections.body_html:
Australian barbecue demands knives that can handle everything from thick brisket flats to delicate lamb cutlets. BBQ knives need longer blades for clean single-pass slices through large cuts, enough rigidity to maintain control through dense muscle, and edge retention that lasts through an entire cook session without dulling.

The essential BBQ knife lineup starts with a long slicing knife (10-12 inches) for brisket and roasts, adds a sturdy chef knife for breaking down larger pieces, and finishes with a boning knife for trimming fat and separating joints. Many Australian BBQ enthusiasts also keep a Chinese cleaver on hand for chopping through ribs and poultry.

Our BBQ-suitable knives include slicing knives from the Lan Series (powder steel for extreme edge retention) and the Supreme Series (German-style steel for low-maintenance durability through long smoking sessions).

BBQ Knife Guides

For dedicated brisket-slicing advice, read our brisket slicing knife guide. Our meat carving guide covers blade length, flexibility, and technique for all protein types. If you're interested in the Japanese approach to meat slicing, see our sujihiki knife guide.

Browse also: Slicing Knives | Carving Forks | Chef Knives

collections.title: Best Knives Under $100
collections.body_html:
Quality kitchen knives under $100 don't have to mean compromising on performance. Our entry-level range uses the same high-carbon stainless steels and construction standards as our premium lines, with simpler handle materials and finishes keeping prices accessible. Every knife in this range outperforms the mass-market blades sold in department stores and kitchenware chains.

At this price point, the smartest buy is a single versatile knife rather than a cheap set. An 8-inch chef knife or 7-inch santoku from the Yu Series covers most kitchen tasks and gives you a genuine feel for what a properly made knife can do.

Buying Guides

Our best knives for every budget guide includes specific recommendations at each price tier. For help choosing your first quality knife, see the beginner's buying guide.

Browse also: Under $150 | Under $200 | Best Starter Knives

image.alt: Best Knives Under $100 - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Best Knives Under $150
collections.body_html:
The $100-$150 range is where kitchen knives start to get exciting. At this price point, you gain access to harder steels, better edge retention, and more refined handle construction. You'll find VG10 and 10Cr15CoMoV steels that hold their edge for weeks of regular home use, plus Damascus-clad options that look as impressive as they perform.

This range includes knives from the Retro Series and selected pieces from the Mo Series. Both offer genuine Damascus cladding and premium handle materials, delivering the kind of performance and aesthetics that traditional brands charge $250 or more for.

Buying Guides

Our best knives for every budget guide breaks down what to expect at each price level. For an understanding of the steels available at this range, see our VG10 vs 10Cr15CoMoV comparison.

Browse also: Under $100 | Under $200 | Damascus Knives

image.alt: Best Knives Under $150 - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Best Knives Under $200
collections.body_html:
Under $200 opens the door to our full Damascus range and selected powder steel knives, blades that compete directly with $300-$500 knives from established Japanese and German brands. At this price point, you're choosing between different steel philosophies and handle styles rather than compromising on quality.

collections.title: Best Starter Kitchen Knives
collections.body_html:

For most beginners, an 8-inch chef knife or 7-inch santoku is the ideal starting point. Both handle chopping, slicing, dicing, and mincing, the tasks you'll do most often. collections.title: PM8S Retro Series
collections.body_html:
The HEZHEN PM8S Retro Series brings a traditional Japanese-influenced aesthetic to everyday kitchen performance. Built around an octagonal wa-style handle in deep red wood and genuine black buffalo horn, this collection is the entry point into Japanese-style knife handling for cooks who want a clean, single-steel blade over a layered Damascus look. Where the HEZHEN PM8O Zhen Series opts for 67-layer Damascus cladding, the PM8S uses a simpler 3-layer composite that keeps the focus on the cutting core and lets the handle do the talking.

3-Layer 10Cr15CoMoV Composite Steel

Each blade in the Retro Series uses a 3-layer composite construction: a 10Cr15CoMoV high-carbon stainless steel core protected on both faces by softer outer layers that add durability and resist flex. This is not Damascus. There is no folded cladding pattern, no layer count to market, just a single high-quality alloy in the position where it counts most, the cutting edge. The outer stainless layers bond tightly to the core during forging, giving the blade good corrosion resistance and toughness without relying on pattern complexity.

If you want to understand how 10Cr15CoMoV compares to Japanese VG-10, the VG-10 vs 10Cr15CoMoV guide covers the alloy differences in detail. The short version: both sit in the same hardness band and sharpen similarly. The core is vacuum heat treated to 60 to 62 HRC, producing a sharp, durable edge that holds up under daily prep without needing frequent attention.

Red Wood and Black Buffalo Horn Octagonal Handle

The Retro Series handle is the centrepiece of the design. Deep red wood provides the main grip, its density and natural oils giving it a warm, smooth feel in the hand, while a genuine black buffalo horn collar sits at the blade end, a detail borrowed directly from traditional Japanese handmade knives. The cross-section is octagonal, the classic wa-style shape that aligns naturally with the fingers and distributes pressure more evenly across the grip than round or D-shaped handles. On longer prep sessions this reduces hand fatigue without forcing any particular hold. The result is a handle that works for both pinch-grip and handle-grip users. The full-tang construction runs the steel through to the base of the handle for solid balance.

Key Features

  • Core Steel: 10Cr15CoMoV high-carbon stainless steel, single-steel composite (not Damascus)
  • Blade Construction: 3-layer composite for edge strength and corrosion resistance
  • Hardness: 60 to 62 HRC (Vacuum Heat Treated)
  • Handle: Red Wood body with Black Buffalo Horn collar, octagonal cross-section
  • Construction: Full-tang for balance and long-term durability
  • Design: Traditional wa-style profile for cooks who prefer a lighter, Japanese-influenced grip

The PM8S Retro Series suits cooks who want the look and feel of a handmade Japanese kitchen knife at an accessible price, without the maintenance demands of reactive high-carbon steel.

image.alt: Retro Series - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Professional Chef Knives
collections.body_html:
Professional-grade kitchen knives prioritise edge retention, balance, and durability through high-volume use. In a commercial kitchen, a chef knife might process hundreds of kilograms of produce per week, the steel needs to hold its edge, the handle needs to resist moisture and heat, and the blade geometry needs to support efficient technique for extended periods without causing fatigue.

What separates a professional knife from a home-cook knife isn't brand prestige, it's metallurgy and construction. Harder steels (60+ HRC) maintain sharper edges between honings, full-tang construction ensures balance and longevity, and ergonomic handles reduce strain during eight-hour shifts. Our professional range uses the same steels found in knives costing two to three times more from traditional Japanese and German brands.

The Lan Series in 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel (62 HRC) is our most performance-oriented line, favoured by cooks who prioritise edge retention above all else. The Supreme Series offers a more forgiving steel for environments where knives face harder use and less careful maintenance.

Professional Knife Guides

Understanding steel hardness is critical for professional knife selection, our steel hardness guide explains the practical implications of HRC ratings. For edge maintenance between full sharpenings, the honing steel guide covers proper technique. Our powder steel guide details the metallurgy behind our highest-performance blades.

collections.title: X02 Supreme Series
collections.body_html:
The HEZHEN X02 Supreme Series is the Western-steel pick of the HEZHEN lineup. Where the other HEZHEN series use folded Damascus or high-carbon composite construction, the Supreme Series uses German 1.4116 stainless steel (X50CrMoV15) -- the same alloy category used by Wusthof and Zwilling. That steel choice produces a knife that is genuinely tough and straightforward to maintain at home, aimed at cooks who want a reliable chef knife without the additional care requirements of a harder steel. Every blade runs a full-tang construction with a classic red wood handle and stainless rivets.

German 1.4116 Stainless Steel (X50CrMoV15)

German 1.4116, also known as X50CrMoV15, is the foundational steel of the Western kitchen knife industry. The composition -- 0.50% carbon, 15% chromium, with molybdenum and vanadium additions -- produces a blade that resists corrosion well and tolerates the full range of daily kitchen use. It sharpens back to a working edge on a standard honing rod without special technique. Heat-treated to 56-58 HRC, this steel sits in the softer range of kitchen knives. That softness is not a weakness: at this hardness the edge is less brittle than harder steels, which means it bends rather than chips if it meets a bone or a hard board edge at an unexpected angle. It also means you can maintain it at home with any steel rod or basic whetstone without worrying about over-grinding a fragile edge.

For cooks considering a step up in edge performance, the X02O Pin Series uses a 3-layer 10Cr15CoMoV composite steel at 60-62 HRC. That extra hardness holds a sharper angle for longer -- but the Supreme Series is more forgiving in everyday use and easier to restore when it dulls.

Classic Red Wood Handle with Stainless Rivets

The handle is shaped from red wood, a dense hardwood selected for its stability and the warm contrast it makes against the satin-finished blade. Three stainless steel rivets fix the full tang in place, distributing handle weight evenly along the grip. The profile fits a standard western pinch hold at the heel, where your index finger and thumb sit across the flat of the blade, and it fills the palm comfortably in a full-wrap grip for tasks that need more force. Red wood handles respond well to occasional oiling to maintain their finish, particularly in dry climates.

Key Features

  • Core Steel: German 1.4116 (X50CrMoV15) stainless steel -- the same alloy used by Wusthof and Zwilling
  • Blade Construction: Solid single-steel blade, no Damascus cladding
  • Hardness: 56-58 HRC (tough, chip-resistant, easy to resharpen at home)
  • Handle: Red wood with stainless steel rivets, full-tang construction
  • Design: Classic Western profile suited to rock-chopping and pinch-grip use

The X02 Supreme Series is the right starting point for a cook who wants a dependable kitchen knife collection built around Western steel -- honest in its construction and straightforward to keep sharp through years of daily use.

image.alt: Supreme Series - Xinzuo Australia

The 3-layer composite construction enhances blade strength while maintaining flexibility and ease of maintenance. Vacuum heat treatment ensures a hardness of 60±2 HRC, providing a razor-sharp edge that stays consistent through repeated use.

Natural Olive Wood Handle

The full-tang construction with riveted design ensures stability and balance, allowing for confident control whether slicing vegetables, trimming meat, or preparing delicate ingredients. Browse our chef knives for more options.

Blade Construction: 3-layer composite steel
Hardness: 60±2 HRC (Vacuum Heat Treated)
Handle: Natural olive wood with full-tang riveted structure
Design: Clean, modern profile for everyday precision

collections.title: X06 Mo Series
collections.body_html:
The Xinzuo X06 Mo Series is the Damascus line built around a single defining feature: a hand-shaped burnt oak handle with a red G10 accent ring that gives each knife a scorched, dark-toned presence unlike anything else in the Xinzuo range. Beneath that distinctive look lies the same 10Cr15CoMoV steel core found in the Yu (B13R) and Elegant (B38L) series, which means cooks choosing between those lines and the Mo Series are choosing on handle character alone. Browse the full Damascus kitchen knife collection to see where the Mo Series sits across the lineup.

High-Performance 10Cr15CoMoV Core

Each X06 blade is built around a 10Cr15CoMoV high-carbon stainless steel core, a composition widely compared to Japanese VG-10 for its strong edge retention and corrosion resistance without demanding complex maintenance. The steel is vacuum heat treated to 60-62 HRC, a hardness range that holds a sharp edge through rigorous daily prep without the brittleness that pushes hardness higher brings. The core is then clad in 67 layers of folded Damascus steel, which strengthens the blade structure and produces the flowing, layered pattern visible across the flats. For more on what separates genuine Damascus construction from decorative patterning, see our guide to real vs. fake Damascus knives.

Burnt Oak Handle with Red G10 Accent

The Mo Series handle is shaped from burnt oak, a hardwood that has been heat-treated to deepen its color and tighten its grain structure. The scorched surface gives each knife a dark, matte finish that stands apart from the lighter, polished wood handles found on the Yu or Master series. A red G10 accent ring at the bolster adds a controlled contrast point without disrupting the overall dark aesthetic. The full-tang construction runs the steel the full length of the handle, and the shaped profile sits naturally in a pinch grip at the heel for confident control through long prep sessions. The handle's lower moisture absorption compared to untreated woods makes it well suited to regular kitchen use.

Key Features

  • Core Steel: 10Cr15CoMoV (VG-10 equivalent) for superior edge retention and corrosion resistance
  • Blade Construction: 67-layer folded Damascus steel for blade strength and visual depth
  • Hardness: 60-62 HRC (Vacuum Heat Treated)
  • Handle: Full-tang burnt oak with red G10 accent ring
  • Design: Dark, scorched-finish aesthetic for cooks who want premium Damascus performance with a bolder handle character

The X06 Mo Series suits cooks who want the same steel foundation as the professional chef knife range but prefer a handle that makes a statement, dark and warm with a build quality that holds up over time.

image.alt: MO Series - Xinzuo Australia

The full-tang construction ensures strength and balance, while the ergonomic shaping provides a secure grip and precise control during extended prep work.

Key Features

High-Performance 10Cr15CoMoV Core

Classic Rosewood Handle

The handle is crafted from premium Rosewood, known for its density, durability, and rich, reddish-brown hues. Unlike synthetic materials, Rosewood offers a warm, organic tactile feel that improves grip and comfort during extended chopping sessions. The handle is shaped for ergonomic control and finished to a smooth polish, highlighting the natural grain of the wood.

Key Features

The B13R Yu Series is the perfect choice for cooks who appreciate the natural beauty of wood handles without compromising on modern cutting performance.

collections.title: B38L Elegant Series
collections.body_html:
The B38L Elegant Series is a masterclass in modern knife design, blending high-performance Japanese-style metallurgy with contemporary aesthetics. This series is defined by its stunning 67-layer Damascus steel blade and a unique, ultra-durable Linen Micarta handle, creating a tool that is as beautiful as it is functional.

Refined Damascus Performance

At the core of every B38L knife is 10Cr15CoMoV steel, a premium high-carbon stainless steel often compared to Japanese VG-10. Learn more in our VG10 vs 10Cr15CoMoV steel guide. Vacuum heat-treated to a hardness of 60±2 HRC, this core delivers exceptional sharpness and edge retention. It is enveloped in 67 layers of folded Damascus steel, creating a mesmerizing, flowing pattern that not only protects the core but reduces food adhesion while slicing.

The Linen Micarta Advantage

What sets the Elegant Series apart is its handle. Crafted from high-grade Linen Micarta, a composite material made from linen cloth and resin, it offers superior durability compared to traditional wood. It is impervious to moisture, resistant to shrinking or cracking, and provides a textured, secure grip even when wet. The handle is finished with a sophisticated mosaic rivet, adding a touch of luxury to its understated design.

Key Features

  • Core Steel: 10Cr15CoMoV (VG-10 equivalent) for long-lasting sharpness.
  • Blade Construction: 67-Layer Damascus Steel for strength and beauty.
  • Hardness: 60±2 HRC (Vacuum Heat Treated & Deep Freeze).
  • Handle: Durable Linen Micarta with Mosaic Rivet.
  • Aesthetics: A perfect balance of classic Damascus patterns and modern, minimalist handle design.

Whether you are a professional chef requiring durability or a home cook seeking elegance, the B38L Series delivers a sophisticated cutting experience that stands the test of time.

image.alt: B38L Elegant Series - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Boning Knives
collections.body_html:
A boning knife is the tool that makes the difference between clean, efficient butchery prep and wasted meat left on the bone. These narrow, curved blades are designed to work around joints, follow contours, and separate meat from bone with minimal waste.

Xinzuo boning knives feature 6\" blades across four series, including a Japanese-style honesuki option in the Zhen series for poultry work. Steel cores include 10Cr15CoMoV and 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel in 67 or 73-layer Damascus cladding, hardened to HRC 60 to 64. Handle materials range from olive wood to rosewood and buffalo horn. If you also work with whole fish, pair a boning knife with one of our fillet knives for more flexible blade action. Both complement a solid chef knife for complete protein prep.

image.alt: Boning Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Bread Knives
collections.body_html:
A proper bread knife makes all the difference. These serrated blades cut cleanly through crusty sourdough, dense rye, and soft sandwich loaves without crushing the crumb. Each blade features long, scalloped teeth designed for smooth, sawing cuts that let the bread do the work, not your arm.

Xinzuo bread knives range from 8\" to 9\" blades across multiple series, with steel cores including 10Cr15CoMoV and 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel (HRC 60 to 64) in 67 or 73-layer Damascus cladding. Handle options include burnt oak, olive wood, and rosewood. Pair one with a quality chef knife and you have most kitchen tasks covered. Also worth considering alongside our slicing knives for complete carving and cutting coverage.

image.alt: Bread Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Bunka Knives
collections.body_html:
The bunka is a Japanese-style knife with a distinctive K-tip profile that has rapidly gained popularity among home cooks and professional chefs alike. Its flat belly keeps the edge in full contact with the cutting board for precise push cuts, while the pointed tip handles fine detail work like scoring, mincing garlic, and breaking down herbs.

Think of it as a more agile alternative to a santoku, with better tip access for intricate tasks. Xinzuo bunka knives feature 67 or 73-layer Damascus cladding over VG10 and 10Cr15CoMoV steel cores, hardened to HRC 60 to 62. If you do a lot of vegetable prep, pair one with a nakiri for the ultimate board setup. New to this blade style? Our bunka knife guide covers everything you need to know.

image.alt: Bunka Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Carving Forks
collections.body_html:
A carving fork is the essential partner to any slicing knife. Xinzuo carving forks feature long, sturdy tines that anchor roasts, hams, and poultry firmly while you carve. The Damascus steel bolsters and ergonomic handles are designed to match your Xinzuo knife collection for a cohesive set at the table.

Pair with our slicing knives for clean, even portions, or explore BBQ knives for outdoor grilling.

collections.title: Chef Knives
collections.body_html:
A chef knife is the backbone of any kitchen. Whether you're breaking down a whole chicken, mincing garlic, or slicing through dense root vegetables, a well-made chef knife handles roughly 90% of your cutting tasks. The broad, curved blade allows for efficient rocking motions, while the pointed tip manages precision work like scoring and trimming.

Choosing the right chef knife means balancing blade length, steel hardness, and handle comfort against your cooking style. Home cooks typically prefer 8-inch blades for manoeuvrability, while experienced cooks often move up to 8.5 or 10-inch blades for greater efficiency on larger cutting boards. High-carbon steels like VG10 and 10Cr15CoMoV hold sharper edges longer than softer German-style steels, but they require more careful maintenance.

Our chef knives span five series, each built for different priorities. The Supreme Series delivers professional-grade performance in German-style stainless steel. The Mo Series pairs 67-layer Damascus cladding with a VG10 core for striking aesthetics without sacrificing edge retention. For powder metallurgy performance, the Lan Series uses 14Cr14MoVNb steel rated at 62 HRC.

Choosing Your Chef Knife

Not sure where to start? Our complete chef knife buying guide walks through steel types, blade profiles, and handle preferences. If you're deciding between Western and Japanese styles, the santoku vs chef knife comparison clarifies the key differences. For those building a full set, see our guide on sets vs individual knives.

Browse also: Santoku Knives | Nakiri Knives | Best Starter Knives

image.alt: Chef Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Chinese Cleavers
collections.body_html:
The Chinese cleaver is the most versatile knife in any Chinese kitchen. Despite the broad, rectangular blade, these are not heavy-duty cleavers. They are precision instruments, built for slicing, dicing, mincing, and scooping ingredients off the board. Xinzuo's Chinese cleavers feature high-carbon steel cores with 67-layer Damascus cladding, giving you a thin, sharp edge that holds up through long prep sessions.

If you are looking for a Western-style all-rounder, browse our chef knives. For a Japanese-style vegetable knife with a similar flat profile, see our nakiri knives.

image.alt: Chinese Cleavers - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Cleavers
collections.body_html:
Built for heavy-duty work, Xinzuo kitchen cleavers handle jobs that would damage a standard knife. Use them for splitting poultry, cutting through cartilage, and breaking down bone. The thick spine and weighted blade do the hard work for you, reducing effort and fatigue.

Not to be confused with Chinese cleavers, which are lighter precision knives for slicing and dicing. For everyday cutting tasks, see our chef knives.

image.alt: Cleavers - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Damascus Chef Knives
collections.body_html:
Damascus chef knives combine the versatility of a Western-style chef blade with the visual distinction of layered Damascus steel. The forged layers create patterns that are unique to every knife, no two Damascus chef knives look exactly alike, making each one a personal piece as much as a functional tool.

Performance-wise, our Damascus chef knives use either VG10 or 10Cr15CoMoV core steels for the cutting edge, delivering the same sharpness and edge retention you'd find in single-steel alternatives. The surrounding Damascus layers (67 or 73 layers depending on the series) add corrosion resistance and structural support without affecting cutting geometry.

The Mo Series Damascus chef knife is our most popular, pairing 67-layer Damascus with an ergonomic Western handle. For a different aesthetic, the Retro Series offers a more traditional Japanese handle profile with 10Cr15CoMoV Damascus cladding.

Damascus Guides

Learn how to identify genuine Damascus craftsmanship in our real vs fake Damascus guide. For care specific to Damascus blades, including pattern preservation, see Damascus steel care. Our complete chef knife buying guide compares Damascus against other steel options.

Browse also: All Damascus Knives | Damascus Knife Sets | All Chef Knives

collections.title: Damascus Kitchen Knives
collections.body_html:
Damascus kitchen knives combine centuries-old layered steel craftsmanship with modern metallurgy. Each blade is forged from multiple layers of steel (typically 67 or 73 layers) folded and hammered to create the distinctive wavy pattern that makes every knife unique. Beyond aesthetics, the layering process produces a blade with a hard cutting core protected by softer, more resilient outer layers.

The visual appeal of Damascus steel is undeniable, but performance matters more in a working kitchen. Our Damascus knives use a VG10 or 10Cr15CoMoV core steel capable of holding a sharp edge through extended use, while the surrounding layers add corrosion resistance and shock absorption. You get a knife that looks excellent on a magnetic holder and performs reliably during daily prep.

Understanding Damascus quality means looking beyond the pattern. Genuine Damascus involves actual forge-welding of distinct steel alloys, not cosmetic acid etching on a single-steel blade. The depth and consistency of the pattern indicate manufacturing quality, deeper, more defined patterns generally reflect better forge technique.

Learn More About Damascus Steel

Our guide to identifying real vs fake Damascus explains what separates genuine forged Damascus from imitation patterns. For a deeper comparison of layering approaches, see 67-layer vs 73-layer Damascus. If you're ready to buy, the Damascus buying guide covers pattern types, performance considerations, and pricing.

Browse also: Damascus Chef Knives | Damascus Knife Sets | Mo Series

collections.title: Damascus Knife Sets
collections.body_html:
Building a knife collection one blade at a time gets expensive. These Damascus knife sets bundle our most popular knives together at a better price than buying individually, with matched handles and consistent steel across every piece in the set.

Each set features genuine Damascus cladding, either 67-layer or 73-layer, over high-carbon steel cores like 10Cr15CoMoV and 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel. Sets range from 2-piece essentials (chef knife plus utility knife) to 3-piece configurations that add a santoku or bunka. Available across the Lan and Mo series with handle materials including olive wood and burnt oak. Looking for sets outside the Damascus range? Browse our full knife sets collection. Every set pairs well with a quality chef knife as your daily workhorse.

collections.title: Fillet Knives
collections.body_html:
A good fillet knife makes the difference between clean portions and wasted fish. Xinzuo fillet knives have thin, flexible blades that follow the contours of bone and skin, letting you remove fillets with minimal waste. The high-carbon steel edge stays sharp through repetitive slicing motions, and the narrow blade profile gives you precise control.

Pair with a boning knife for breaking down whole fish and poultry, or browse our slicing knives for carving larger cuts of meat.

image.alt: Fillet Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Gyuto Knives
collections.body_html:
The gyuto is the Japanese answer to the Western chef knife, built for the same versatility but with a thinner blade profile, harder steel, and lighter overall weight. Where a typical European chef knife runs 56 to 58 HRC, our gyuto knives use VG10 and 10Cr15CoMoV cores hardened to 60 to 62 HRC, holding a sharper edge angle of 12 to 15 degrees per side. The result is cleaner cuts with less effort, particularly noticeable on proteins and delicate vegetables.

If you prefer the rocking motion of a traditional Western chef knife, a gyuto still accommodates that style while excelling at push-cutting and slicing. For a shorter, flatter alternative, consider a santoku. New to gyuto knives? Our gyuto knife guide covers blade geometry, steel choices, and how to pick the right size.

image.alt: Gyuto Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Japanese Kitchen Knives
collections.body_html:
Japanese kitchen knives are designed for precision. Thinner blades, harder steels, and more acute edge angles (typically 15 degrees versus 20 degrees for Western knives) allow cleaner cuts with less effort. This matters for ingredients where cell structure affects flavour and presentation, sashimi, herbs, and delicate vegetables all benefit from a sharper, thinner edge.

Japanese-style knife design covers a range of specialised shapes, each built for specific tasks. The santoku is the versatile all-rounder, the nakiri excels at vegetable prep, and the bunka combines a k-tip profile with everyday functionality. For Australian home cooks who want one Japanese-style knife to start with, a santoku or gyuto (Japanese chef knife) is the most practical entry point.

Our Japanese-style knives are made in Yangjiang, China's knife-making capital, using the same high-grade steels (VG10, 10Cr15CoMoV, powder steel) found in premium Japanese brands. The advantage is value, you get equivalent metallurgy and craftsmanship without the heritage markup that inflates traditional Japanese brands.

Japanese-style knife Guides

New to Japanese-style knives? Our Japanese-style knife types guide explains every major blade shape and its ideal use. For an honest comparison of Eastern and Western approaches, see German vs Japanese kitchen knives. If you're considering building a Japanese-style collection, our beginner's buying guide covers the essential first purchases.

Browse also: Japanese-style knife Sets | Damascus Knives | Chef Knives

collections.title: Japanese Knife Sets
collections.body_html:
Japanese-style knife sets are built around the blade shapes that define Japanese kitchen culture: the santoku for versatile prep, the nakiri for dedicated vegetable work, and the petty for precision tasks. Our Japanese-style sets pair these traditional shapes with modern steels and construction, giving Australian home cooks the benefits of Japanese blade geometry at accessible price points.

A typical three-piece Japanese set includes a gyuto or santoku as the primary blade, a utility or petty knife for smaller tasks, and either a nakiri or paring knife to round out the collection. This covers 95% of daily kitchen tasks while keeping the set compact and purposeful.

Our Japanese sets are available in the Mo Series (67-layer Damascus, VG10 core) and the Retro Series (Damascus clad, 10Cr15CoMoV core). Both feature Japanese-inspired blade profiles with handles suited to Western grip styles.

Buying Guides

For a breakdown of essential Japanese blade shapes, read our Japanese-style knife types guide. Deciding between a set and building your own collection? Our sets vs individual knives comparison covers the pros and cons. For first-time buyers, the beginner's buying guide recommends the best starting pieces.

Browse also: All Knife Sets | Japanese-Style Knives | Damascus Knife Sets

collections.title: Kitchen Scissors
collections.body_html:
Good kitchen scissors handle tasks that knives cannot. Use them for spatchcocking poultry, snipping fresh herbs, trimming pastry, or cutting through packaging. Xinzuo kitchen shears are built from high-carbon stainless steel for lasting sharpness and easy cleaning.

Browse our full range of kitchen accessories or explore the complete knife collection.

collections.title: Knife Guards & Sheaths
collections.body_html:
Knife guards protect your blade edges during storage and transport, preventing chips, dulling, and accidental cuts when rummaging through drawers or bags. If you do not use a knife block or magnetic strip, sheaths are essential for keeping your knives in top condition.

Our sheaths are designed to fit specific Xinzuo series, so each guard matches the blade profile precisely. Options cover the Lan (B37), Yu (B13R), Supreme (X02), Mo (X06), Retro (PM8S), and Zhen series. They are especially useful for chefs who travel with their knives or store blades in drawers. For wall-mounted home storage, check our magnetic knife holders. For transporting a full kit, our chef knife roll bags offer dedicated pockets for each blade.

collections.title: Knife Sets
collections.body_html:
A good knife set gives you the essential blades for every kitchen task without the guesswork of building a collection piece by piece. Our sets are designed around how Australian home cooks actually work, starting with a versatile chef or santoku knife, adding a utility knife for mid-size tasks, and including a paring knife for precision work.

Unlike generic department store sets padded with redundant blades, each knife in our sets earns its place. We don't include steak knives, kitchen shears, or novelty pieces to inflate the knife count. Instead, you get three to seven purpose-built knives that cover the full range of cutting, slicing, and prep tasks.

Sets are available across our major series. The Supreme Series sets suit cooks who prioritise low-maintenance German-style steel. For those who want the visual impact of Damascus, the Mo Series and Retro Series sets combine striking blade patterns with premium performance.

Set Buying Guides

Not sure whether a set or individual knives make more sense? Our sets vs individual knives guide compares both approaches. For a broader look at building your ideal kitchen toolkit, see the best kitchen knives for every budget. Looking for a gift? Our kitchen knife gift guide includes set recommendations by price range.

Browse also: Japanese Knife Sets | Damascus Knife Sets | Best Starter Knives

collections.title: Knife Stands & Blocks
collections.body_html:
Freestanding knife storage is ideal for kitchens where wall-mounting is not an option. Xinzuo knife stands and blocks keep your blades organised, accessible, and protected from edge damage. They suit any benchtop layout and hold multiple knife sizes securely.

For wall-mounted options, see our magnetic knife holders. Protect individual blades during transport with our knife guards.

image.alt: Knife Stands & Blocks - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Magnetic Knife Holders
collections.body_html:
Magnetic knife holders keep your blades visible, accessible, and safer than a drawer full of loose knives. Wall-mounted strips use strong magnets to hold knives securely, freeing up counter space while letting blade edges air-dry properly after washing.

Unlike traditional knife blocks, magnetic holders do not trap moisture or let blades rub against each other, which helps maintain edge sharpness between sharpening sessions. Our range includes wall-mounted strips in 40cm and 50cm lengths, plus freestanding magnetic blocks for benchtop use. Materials include acacia and walnut wood options from Hezhen alongside double-sided freestanding designs. Pair with knife guards for travel, or browse our knife stands for alternative storage. For help choosing, read our knife block buying guide.

collections.title: Nakiri Knives
collections.body_html:
The nakiri is Japan's dedicated vegetable knife. Its flat, rectangular blade makes full contact with the cutting board on every stroke, producing clean, precise cuts without the rocking motion required by curved Western blades. For cooks who do serious vegetable prep (from fine julienne to thick-cut stir-fry pieces) the nakiri is faster and more efficient than a chef knife for these tasks.

The double-bevel edge (sharpened on both sides) makes the nakiri accessible to both right- and left-handed cooks, unlike single-bevel Japanese-style knives that require specific orientation. The thin blade profile creates minimal resistance through dense vegetables like pumpkin and beetroot, reducing the effort needed for repetitive cutting.

Our nakiri knives range from the affordable Yu Series to the premium Lan Series in powder steel. The classic 7-inch blade length suits most cutting boards and hand sizes.

Nakiri Guides

For a complete breakdown of how the nakiri compares to other vegetable-cutting options, read our nakiri knife guide. The best knives for vegetables article compares the nakiri against the santoku and vegetable cleaver. For care tips specific to thin Japanese blades, see our daily maintenance guide.

Browse also: Santoku Knives | Chinese Cleavers | Bunka Knives

image.alt: Nakiri Knives - Xinzuo Australia
New Arrivals - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Paring Knives
collections.body_html:
A paring knife handles the detail work that larger blades cannot manage. With 3 to 4 inch blades forged from the same Damascus-clad, high-carbon steel as our full-size knives, these are precision tools for peeling fruit, deveining prawns, hulling strawberries, and trimming fine garnishes. The short blade gives you fingertip control that no chef knife can match.

Pair a paring knife with an 8-inch chef knife and you have roughly 80% of your kitchen cutting covered. For jobs that fall between the two, a 5 to 6 inch utility knife fills the gap perfectly.

image.alt: Paring Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: PM8M Zhen Series
collections.body_html:
The Legend of Japanese Carbon Steel

The PM8M Zhen Series is crafted for the culinary purist. At its heart lies the legendary Aogami #2 (Blue Paper Steel) from Hitachi Metals, Japan. Revered by sushi chefs and knife enthusiasts worldwide, this high-carbon steel offers a level of sharpness and "cutting feel" that stainless steels simply cannot match.

Why Choose the PM8M Blue Steel Series?

  • Aogami #2 Core (62-64 HRC): This series features a core of Japanese Blue Steel No. 2. Infused with tungsten and chromium, it achieves a high hardness of 62-64 HRC. This allows for an ultra-thin, razor-sharp edge that glides through ingredients with zero resistance.
  • Superior Sharpening Feel: One of the greatest benefits of Aogami #2 is how easily it takes a new edge. Unlike hard stainless steels which can feel "glassy" on a whetstone, Blue Steel offers excellent tactile feedback, making it a joy to sharpen and maintain.
  • Stainless Cladding (San Mai): To make maintenance easier, the reactive high-carbon core is clad in layers of softer stainless steel. This "San Mai" construction protects the majority of the blade from rust, exposing only the cutting edge to the elements.
  • Figured Maple Wood Handle: The handle is crafted from premium North American Maple Wood, prized for its durability and beautiful, wavy grain. It is shaped into an octagonal Wa-style design and finished with a buffalo horn bolster for perfect balance.

Technical Specifications

Core Material Aogami #2 (Japanese Blue Paper Steel)
Construction San Mai (Stainless Clad Carbon)
Hardness 62-64 HRC
Handle Material North American Maple Wood & Buffalo Horn
Edge Angle 15° per side
⚠️ Care Warning: This knife features a High-Carbon Steel edge. It is NOT fully stainless. You must wipe the blade dry immediately after washing to prevent rust. Over time, the exposed edge will develop a unique blue-grey patina, this is normal and shows the knife's history.

image.alt: PM8M Zhen Series - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: PM8O Zhen Series
collections.body_html:
Where Traditional Artistry Meets High-Carbon Performance

The PM8O Zhen Series represents the pinnacle of Xinzuo’s craftsmanship, designed for professional chefs and culinary enthusiasts. This series blends the proven performance of 10Cr15CoMoV High-Carbon Steel (VG10 equivalent) with the timeless elegance of traditional Wa-style handles.

Why Choose the PM8O Zhen Series?

  • Premium High-Carbon Core: At the heart of every blade is a 10Cr15CoMoV steel core. This premium composition mirrors the properties of Japanese VG10, offering an exceptional balance of edge retention, stain resistance, and ease of sharpening. Heat-treated to 60-62 HRC, it maintains a razor-sharp edge through rigorous daily use.
  • Stunning Damascus Cladding: The core is encased in 67 layers of Damascus steel, creating a mesmerizing, fluid pattern unique to every knife. This folded steel cladding not only adds breathtaking beauty but also provides durability and protection to the cutting core.
  • Masterful Hand-Polishing: Each knife is hand-finished using traditional methods, sharpening the edge to a laser-like 15° angle per side. The result is effortless slicing that preserves the texture and freshness of your ingredients.
  • Ergonomic "Wa" Handle: The handle is a work of art in itself, crafted from premium North American Olive Wood paired with a Black Buffalo Horn bolster. The octagonal shape fits naturally in the hand, offering superior grip and balance for fatigue-free cutting.

Technical Specifications

Core Material 10Cr15CoMoV (VG10 Equivalent)
Layering 67-Layer Damascus
Hardness 60-62 HRC
Handle Material North American Olive Wood & Buffalo Horn
Edge Angle 15° per side

image.alt: PM8O Zhen Series - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Santoku Knives
collections.body_html:
The santoku is Japan's answer to the Western chef knife, a versatile blade designed for the "three virtues" of slicing, dicing, and mincing. Its flatter profile and shorter length (typically 7 inches) make it ideal for cooks who prefer a push-cut or up-and-down chopping motion rather than the rocking technique used with curved Western blades.

Where the santoku excels is vegetable preparation. The wide, flat blade creates full contact with the cutting board, producing clean cuts through everything from paper-thin cucumber slices to dense sweet potato rounds. The lighter weight compared to a Western chef knife also reduces fatigue during extended prep sessions.

Our santoku knives are available across multiple series, from the entry-level Yi Series in German-style steel to the Mo Series in 67-layer Damascus. Each shares the classic santoku geometry (flat edge, sheepsfoot tip, wide blade) while the steel and handle construction vary to suit different budgets and preferences.

Santoku Guides

Deciding between a santoku and a chef knife? Our santoku vs chef knife comparison breaks down the practical differences. For dedicated vegetable prep, you might also consider the nakiri, our guide compares the two approaches. Learn more about cutting techniques in our knife techniques guide.

Browse also: Chef Knives | Nakiri Knives | Best Starter Knives

image.alt: Santoku Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Slicing Knives
collections.body_html:
Slicing knives are built for one job: long, smooth, single-pass cuts through roasts, brisket, ham, and sashimi. The narrow blade reduces friction and drag, letting you carve thin, even slices without tearing or shredding the meat.

Xinzuo slicing knives (also called carving knives) come in 8\" and 10\" lengths across five series. Steel cores include 10Cr15CoMoV and 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel with 67 or 73-layer Damascus cladding, hardened to HRC 60 to 64. The longer 10\" blades are ideal for large roasts and brisket, while the 8\" versions suit everyday carving. Essential for weekend BBQ sessions and Sunday roast prep. Pair with a versatile chef knife for a well-rounded kitchen setup.

image.alt: Slicing Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Steak Knives
collections.body_html:
Xinzuo Damascus steel steak knives bring the same craftsmanship from the kitchen to the dining table. Each knife features a razor-sharp edge and matched handle design, giving your table setting a cohesive, premium look. The fine edge cuts through steak cleanly without tearing, keeping juices where they belong.

Looking for a complete set? Browse our knife sets or check the best sellers for our most popular pieces.

image.alt: Steak Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: Utility Knives
collections.body_html:
The utility knife sits between a chef knife and a paring knife, typically 5 to 6 inches long, making it the ideal in-between blade. It handles jobs too precise for a full-size chef knife but too large for a paring knife: slicing tomatoes, portioning sandwich fillings, trimming meat, and cutting citrus. Many home cooks reach for their utility knife more than any other blade.

Our utility knives feature the same high-carbon steel cores and 67-layer Damascus cladding as the rest of the range, with HRC ratings of 60 to 62 for long-lasting sharpness. The narrower blade profile makes them lighter and more agile in the hand, ideal for extended prep sessions.

image.alt: Utility Knives - Xinzuo Australia

collections.title: VG10 Steel Knives
collections.body_html:
VG10 is one of the most respected kitchen knife steels in the world, originally developed by Japan's Takefu Special Steel for high-performance cutlery. With a hardness of 60-61 HRC, it holds a sharp edge significantly longer than softer German steels while remaining easier to sharpen than harder powder steels. It's the sweet spot between edge retention and practical maintenance.

VG10 contains vanadium for wear resistance, cobalt for hardness at high temperatures, and molybdenum for corrosion resistance. This combination means VG10 knives resist rust in humid Australian kitchens while maintaining a keen edge through weeks of regular use between sharpenings.

Our VG10 knives appear across several series, including the Mo Series (67-layer Damascus clad) and the Retro Series. Each uses a VG10 core for the cutting edge, surrounded by softer cladding layers that protect the core from lateral stress.

Steel Guides

For a detailed comparison of VG10 against its Chinese counterpart, read our VG10 vs 10Cr15CoMoV guide. Our steel hardness guide explains what HRC ratings mean in practice. For maintenance tips specific to high-carbon stainless steels, see the daily care guide.

Browse also: Damascus Knives | Japanese-Style Knives | Professional Chef Knives

collections.title: Whetstones & Sharpening Stones
collections.body_html:
Every knife dulls with use, and a whetstone is the most effective way to restore a factory-sharp edge. Our combination sharpening stones offer dual grit surfaces, typically 1000 grit for reprofiling and edge repair, and 6000 grit for polishing and refining. This two-stone approach covers both routine maintenance and bringing back a neglected edge.

Damascus steel particularly benefits from whetstone sharpening. The layered construction means each honing session reveals fresh pattern detail while restoring cutting performance. For step-by-step technique, read our whetstone sharpening guide. Browse the full knife collection to find blades worth keeping sharp.

collections.title: X03 Uliassi Series
collections.body_html:
The Xinzuo X03 Uliassi Series is built in direct collaboration with Mauro Uliassi, a three-Michelin-star chef whose restaurant on the Adriatic coast has held its stars since 2012. The knives in this collection were developed to meet the specific demands of a professional kitchen where edge retention and consistent geometry matter across hundreds of covers a service. For home cooks who want the closest thing to a working chef's tool within the Xinzuo range, this is the series to choose. Browse the full Damascus kitchen knife collection to see where it sits in the lineup.

Premium 14Cr14MoVNb Powder Steel Core

The blade core is 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel, produced through powder metallurgy rather than conventional forging. Conventional forged steel has an uneven grain structure because carbon and alloying elements distribute unevenly as the melt cools. Powder metallurgy starts with pre-alloyed powder sintered under controlled pressure, producing a far more uniform grain. That finer structure lets the X03 reach 62 HRC without the brittleness risk that hardness level carries in a forged blade, and it holds the edge longer through demanding prep. This same alloy underpins the Lan Series (B37), which pairs it with olive wood handles and full-tang construction for a different series identity.

73-Layer Damascus Cladding

The powder steel core is wrapped in 73 layers of Damascus cladding, six more than the 67-layer construction used across the rest of the Xinzuo damascus range. The additional layers produce a visibly denser wave pattern on the flats of the blade, and the tighter fold structure adds chip resistance along the shoulders where the cladding meets the core. Each knife is hand-polished to bring out the contrast between the softer outer layers and the harder core, with the pattern running the full length of the blade from heel to tip.

Rosewood Handle, Hidden Tang

The handle is shaped from Rosewood, a dense hardwood with a rich reddish-brown grain and a natural resistance to moisture absorption that makes it stable across repeated wash-and-dry cycles. The hidden tang construction draws the handle material flush against the blade, giving the knife a clean, integrated profile without exposed rivets or bolsters. The grip sits naturally in a pinch hold at the heel, which is how a chef knife is meant to be used for extended prep, and the weight distribution is biased forward to reduce hand fatigue on long sessions.

Key Features

  • Core Steel: 14Cr14MoVNb powder steel for uniform grain, finer edge geometry, and superior edge retention
  • Blade Construction: 73-layer Damascus cladding for chip resistance and a visibly denser pattern
  • Hardness: 62 HRC (Vacuum Heat Treated)
  • Handle: Rosewood, hidden tang, no exposed rivets
  • Design: Michelin-chef collaboration, developed for professional kitchen demands

The X03 Uliassi Series is built for cooks who want a knife shaped by professional requirements, with a powder steel core that holds its edge through serious prep and a Damascus pattern that rewards closer inspection.

collections.title: X05Z Zhen Series
collections.body_html:
The Pinnacle of Hardness: ZDP-189 Powder Steel

The Zhen Series (X05Z) is the crown jewel of the Xinzuo collection, engineered for the connoisseur who demands the absolute maximum in edge retention. This series utilizes ZDP-189, a legendary Japanese powder metallurgy steel known for its extreme hardness.

Why the X05Z Series is Unique

  • Extreme Hardness (65-67 HRC): At the core lies the ultra-premium ZDP-189 Powder Steel. With a carbon content of 3% and chromium of 20%, this steel achieves a staggering hardness of 65-67 HRC. This allows for an edge that is significantly sharper and lasts longer than almost any other steel on the market.
  • 3-Layer San Mai Construction: To protect the ultra-hard core, the blade is crafted using a San Mai (3-layer) Composite technique. Two layers of softer, corrosion-resistant stainless steel sandwich the ZDP-189 core, providing essential durability and preventing brittleness.
  • Rare Desert Ironwood Handle: Deviating from standard woods, the X05Z features a handle made from North American Desert Ironwood, one of the densest and most stable woods in the world. It is impervious to moisture and highly resistant to shrinking or cracking.
  • White Buffalo Horn Bolster: The handle is finished with a stunning White Buffalo Horn bolster, creating a beautiful contrast against the dark Ironwood.

Technical Specifications

Core Material ZDP-189 Powder Steel (Japanese)
Structure 3-Layer Composite (San Mai)
Hardness 65-67 HRC
Handle Material Desert Ironwood & White Buffalo Horn
Edge Angle 15° per side

*Note: Due to the extreme hardness of ZDP-189, these knives are recommended for experienced users. See our whetstone sharpening guide for care tips. Avoid twisting the blade or cutting bone/frozen foods.

image.alt: X05Z Zhen Series - Xinzuo Australia

pages.title: About the Author
pages.body_html:
Dylan Tollemache

Founder and Australian Distributor, Xinzuo Australia

Dylan runs Xinzuo Australia from Wacol, Queensland, with his wife. He is the official Australian distributor for both Xinzuo and Hezhen kitchen knives, working directly with the manufacturing team in Yangjiang, China.

Dylan brings over 15 years of e-commerce experience in product sourcing and brand building. That background taught him how to evaluate manufacturing quality, build supply chains, and cut through marketing noise to find products that actually deliver.

He has visited the Xinzuo factory in Yangjiang multiple times, walking the production line from raw steel billets through to final edge sharpening. He has tested every knife in the range personally, from the entry-level starter knives through to the premium professional chef knives. His articles on this site draw on that first-hand experience, combined with peer-reviewed metallurgy research, manufacturer data, and real-world kitchen testing.

Every article published on xinzuo.com.au is written or reviewed by Dylan. The goal is straightforward: give Australian home cooks and professionals the factual, specific information they need to choose the right knife. No generic filler, no exaggerated claims. If a $60 knife does the job, he will tell you that instead of pushing the $300 option.

When he is not writing about knife steel hardness or Damascus steel construction, Dylan is usually testing new knife prototypes, running the warehouse, or answering customer emails.

Contact: support@xinzuo.com.au

Located: 59 Nashos Pl, Wacol QLD 4076, Australia

pages.title: About Xinzuo Australia
pages.body_html:
Our Story

Xinzuo Australia started with a sharp knife and a bit of obsession.

I'm Dylan, and I've been sharpening knives for years. What began as a hobby quickly became a full-time passion. I'd spend hours testing edges, comparing steels, trying to find that perfect balance between hardness and ease of maintenance. When I started looking at high-end Japanese-style knives, I hit the same wall most Australians do: everything was either wildly overpriced or, worse, cheap steel dressed up with a laser-etched "Damascus" pattern that fooled you into thinking you were getting something premium.

Then I ordered a Xinzuo knife. It sharpened beautifully. It held its edge longer than anything I'd used before. The Damascus pattern was real layered steel, not cosmetic etching. The balance was spot-on, the handle comfortable, and the price made no sense for the quality I was holding. I knew immediately that Australian home cooks and chefs needed access to these knives.

I reached out to Xinzuo directly and became their official Australian distributor. Today, my wife Samantha and I run Xinzuo Australia from Queensland as a small, family-owned business. We personally handle every order, every customer question, and every warranty claim. When you email us, you're talking to one of us, not a call centre.

Inside the Xinzuo Gigafactory

Before agreeing to partner with Xinzuo, I flew to Yangjiang, China to visit their factory in person. Yangjiang is the knife-making capital of the world, and what I found there surprised me.

The Xinzuo Gigafactory is a 15,000-square-metre facility with over 230 staff. The warehouse is fully air-conditioned (a rarity in manufacturing) and runs on approximately 70% solar power. The owners eat in the same cafeteria as every other staff member. There's no executive dining room, no separation. It's one of the most wholesome workplaces I've seen in any industry.

Every Xinzuo knife passes through a 32-step production process. Handles are hand-carved, not injection-moulded. Each blade goes through a four-step hand-honing process to achieve its final edge. The Damascus patterns you see on our knives are created through real layering of steel (67 or 73 layers folded and forged together), never laser-etched imitations.

Quality Control

Xinzuo is obsessive about measurable quality:

  • Laser edge measurement - every blade is tested for edge geometry and angle consistency
  • Rockwell hardness testing - every batch of steel is verified to meet the published HRC rating
  • Precision cutting machines - test durability and edge retention under controlled conditions
  • Weight and balance checks - each knife is weighed and balanced before it leaves the factory
  • Vacuum heat treatment and cryogenic freezing - makes the steel 20-30% harder and more durable than standard methods

When Xinzuo labels a steel, that's exactly what you're getting. They publish the hardness (Rockwell tested), the spine thickness, the behind-the-edge thickness, the exact steel composition, and where it was sourced. Most knife companies won't tell you any of this. Xinzuo puts it all front and centre because they have nothing to hide.

Innovation

One of the things that struck me most about visiting the Gigafactory was Steve Cheng's desk. Steve is Xinzuo's co-founder and Head of Design, and his desk was covered in sketches, prototypes, and test pieces. Different steels, different weights, different handle shapes. Every new Xinzuo design starts as a hand-drawn sketch. From there, the team uses 3D printing to develop and refine concepts before they ever touch real steel.

They've even developed proprietary steels you won't find anywhere else. The Lan Series uses a custom powdered core steel (14Cr14MoVNb) that delivers 62-64 HRC hardness, rivalling steels that cost three times as much. That kind of innovation only comes from people who actually care about making better knives. Their work has been recognised internationally too. The Hezhen X01 Flagship Series won a Gold Prize at the 2024 London Design Awards for product design.

Why "Made in China" Is Something We're Proud Of

We know the reputation. And so does Xinzuo. They openly advertise "Made in China" because they're one of the companies actively working to change what that label means. Yangjiang has been a centre for blade-making for over 1,500 years, longer than most countries have existed. Xinzuo is leading the way in quality control and honest manufacturing at a time when many brands hide their origins behind vague labels like "Japanese-inspired" or "German-engineered."

Most knife companies, even premium brands, won't tell you where their steel actually comes from or how their knives are actually made. Xinzuo tells you everything: the exact steel grade, the heat treatment method, the number of Damascus layers, and the specific hardness of every knife. That kind of honesty is rare, and it's why we work with them.

Read more about modern knife manufacturing in our article: The Truth About Chinese Kitchen Knives (And How to Spot the Good Ones).

Our Promise

Every knife we sell is one we'd use in our own kitchen, and we do, daily. We stand behind every product with a lifetime warranty on manufacturing defects, fast dispatch from Queensland, and a returns policy aligned with Australian Consumer Law.

We're not a massive corporation. We're two people who believe great knives shouldn't cost a fortune, and that you deserve to know exactly what you're buying. If you have any questions about our knives, our story, or which knife is right for you, just reach out. We'd love to help.

Meet the Xinzuo Team

We've worked closely with the Xinzuo team since day one, from visiting the Gigafactory in Yangjiang to exhibiting together at the AGHA Melbourne Gift Fair. The founders aren't distant executives. They're hands-on craftspeople who know every product inside and out. It's a partnership built on a shared obsession with making better knives.

Explore Our Range

Not sure where to start? Here are some helpful resources:

  • Xinzuo Knife Series Comparison - side-by-side breakdown of every series
  • How to Choose a Chef Knife - our complete buying guide
  • Best Starter Knives - our picks for beginners
  • Chef Knives - our most popular category

This Cookie Policy explains how Xinzuo uses cookies and similar technologies on our website.

1. What Are Cookies?
Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They help the site function properly, improve user experience, and provide insights into how the website is used.

2. How We Use Cookies
We use cookies to:

  • Enable essential website functions such as shopping cart and checkout
  • Remember your preferences and settings
  • Improve website performance and functionality
  • Analyze traffic and usage for website improvement
  • Support marketing and advertising efforts (such as Google Analytics or Meta Ads)

3. Types of Cookies We Use

  • Essential cookies - required for the website to work properly.
  • Performance cookies - help us understand how visitors use our site.
  • Functional cookies - remember your preferences.
  • Advertising cookies - used to deliver relevant ads and measure performance.

 

4. Managing Cookies
You can accept or reject cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers automatically accept cookies, but you can modify your settings to decline them. Disabling cookies may affect some website functionality.
